status.dro_move_speed_tooltip:
When a file is running, the move-speed/feedrate of the moving axis is
shown. When your tool is idle, the default X/Y speed/feedrate is shown.

Override will decrease or increase the assigned speed in a file (down
to 5%, up to 200%; with 100% being the assigned speed.).
status.dro_spindle_tooltip:
Set spindle RPM. Gray/Black values indicate commanded RPM; Blue
values indicate Achieved RPM.

AMP value displays the spindle load expressed as current in amps.

actions.jp_tooltip: Goto Park Location (Macro C#79; Settings in C#210)
actions.pause_feedhold_tooltip:
When a file is running, Pause/Feedhold will execute a controlled stop
allowing the file to be subsequently Resumed or Fully Quit.

If an immediate Emergency Stop (E-Stop) is needed, use the physical
RED Button/Lever to brake and shutdown the equipment. The tool's
position will be lost and your file will need to be restarted.
On some tools, the E-Stop may also be the main power switch.

machine.cannot_arm_from_state: Cannot arm machine for {action} from the {state} state

toolbox.tmpdir_failed: Could not create temp directory for toolbox: {error}
toolbox.tmpfile_write_failed: Could not write toolbox temp file: {error}

general.machine_name_tooltip:
The name of your tool. The tool name will be displayed on the dashboard.
It is used as the Wifi name for AP mode and IP address display.
You can change the name here. The display will update the next time
you start the software.
general.machine_profile_tooltip:
The Machine Profile sets the default values, apps and macros for your tool.
Changing this setting will restart FabMo and reset Configurations.
general.unit_system_tooltip: The distance units that will be used for motion and display and in supported apps.
general.distance_mode_tooltip:
Absolute distance means the tool moves to specified coordinates.
Relative distance means the tool moves from its current position
-by- the specified distance. (Relative is rarely used in modern CNC.)
This setting applies primarily to g-code files. Relative can be
applied if needed in an OpenSBP file by using the [SR] command.
general.language_tooltip:
Display language for the dashboard.
Changing this setting reloads the page so the new
translations take effect.

logging.log_level_tooltip:
Determines how aggressively the tool records diagnostic
information in its log.
This should be set to 'Informational' except in certain
tech support situations.
Setting this value to something other than 'Informational'
may negatively impact tool performance.
logging.status_report_interval_tooltip:
The minimum interval between tool status reports.
A larger number here means position and status displays
will not update as frequently [range 100 - 500].

software.firmware_loading_tooltip:
These are firmware load functions for special use cases:
  Either to upload a firmware file you have been provided by tech support,
  or to reload the current firmare in cases where there is a mismatch
  between the SD card and the Control card (e.g. SD card replacement).
software.firmware_upload: SPECIAL |  Update G2 Firmware with a file provided by tech support
software.firmware_reload: SPECIAL |  Reload G2 Firmware from current FabMo

axes.legend_tooltip:
This page contains default/current settings for Axis characteristics:

Feedrate Maximum: The maximum allowable feedrate for this machine in a move
    speed command ([M] commands in OpenSBP or G1 commands in g-code).
Jog Velocity: The speed of Jog or rapid-traverse comands ([J]og commands
   in OpenSBP; G0 command in g-code). May be greater than Feedrate Maximum.
Maximum Jerk: "Jerk" is technically the 3rd derivative of position -- it is the
   rate of change in acceleration/deceleration and determines how quickly your tool changes
   from one speed/direction to another. A higher value here creates more aggressive motion.
   [recommended settings: in inches 50 - 250; in mm 1270 - 6350 ((units/ min³) * 1,000,000)]
   FabMo calculates a smooth, S-shaped speed curve that limits jerk to this setting.
High Brake Jerk: The "jerk" setting creating an abrupt stop for special
   situations such as homing and unprogrammed STOPS. If this value is too
   high or loads heavy, position accuracy may be lost.
Axis Mode: The movement mode for this axis. Linear axes are controlled in the
   default linear unit system. Rotary axes are controlled in degrees of rotation.
   Note that changing the Axis Mode will reset the default parameters for the axis.
axes.axis_mode_abc_tooltip:
Axis Mode for this axis:   Resetting mode resets Axis defaults.
   Default Linear values are estimated from the Z axis.
   Default Rotary values are based on DT-Indexer.
   Move speeds are set to 1/2 of the Feedrate Maximum.
   Please also check the Unit Value for Channel in Drivers.
   Changes made to values will persist unless mode reset.

drivers.channel_tooltip:
These settings provide the lowest level assignment for
each motor output driver channel. The driver channel is
assigned an Axis name, a direction, and a Unit Value.
The Unit Value is the number of steps (controller pulses)
per unit of distance (inches, mm, or degrees). The value
is responsible for setting how far the tool travels per
requested move. If a tool is consistently travelling a
proportionally incorrect distance, the Unit Value is
likely incorrect.

For ShopBot's Desktop tools, the correct Unit Value is:
    4000 steps/inch | 157.48031 steps/mm

inputs.input_tooltip: These settings define the functionality of each of your tools Input lines
inputs.enabled_tooltip: Determines whether the Input is enabled or not.
inputs.action_tooltip:
Defines the action of the Input:

  None: No specific action but icon will display when active.
  Stop: Tool will come to a controlled Stop if Input is activated.
  Stop-Fast: Tool will stop quickly using the High-Brake Jerk setting.
  Interlock: Input will function as a Safety Interlock.
  Limit: Input will function as a Limit switch.
  *Stop-Immediate: Instantaneous stop, special use cases only. Can lose location.
inputs.polarity_tooltip:
Voltage level for activation.
High = Normally low; Active when changed to High.
Low = Normally high; Active when changed to Low.

machine.envelope_tooltip:
The minimum and maximum positions on each axis.
These values define the working limits of the tool
and are used as software limits. Mechanical stops
and homing sensors are usually placed slightly
outside these limits.
machine.enforce_softlimits_tooltip:
When checked, manual jogging stops at the envelope
boundary in work coordinates. Uncheck to allow
jogging past the envelope (e.g. for setup or
recovery). [default=unchecked]
machine.zlift_height_tooltip:
The pull-up height automatically moved to when tool is stopped or
paused. This height may be different than the safe traversing
height set within a file.
If your work sets the table surface as your Z-zero, make sure
the value set here will pull your tool above the material.
[default=0.5]
machine.chordal_tolerance_tooltip:
When an arc segment is being computed from code, this is the
maximum deviation that a segment may take from its "ideal"
trajectory. A smaller number here means finer segments and higher
resolution, but possibly slower execution. [default=0.001]
machine.junction_integration_time_tooltip:
Determines how aggressively the machine takes corners.
Values should be between 0.5 and 2.0. Higher values
mean faster and more aggressive corners. [default=1.5]
machine.spindle_delay_tooltip:
The amount of time allowed for the spindle to spin-up
before motion starts. This interval is applied when the
spindle starts (or restarts) to delay when the tool moves.
[default=2]
machine.planning_mode_tooltip:
Selects the planning mode for the machine. 2D-mode
prioritizes maintaining constant feedrate in XY. 3D-mode
prioritizes maintaining constant reedrate in XYZ. 2D-mode
can be used to minimize dwell in linear motions for tabs
and and to optimize 3D raster carving. [default=3D]
machine.xy_speed_tooltip:
The initial XY speed used when driving the tool with
the Motion Keypad (yellow icon). [adjustable]
machine.min_xy_speed_tooltip:
The the lowest speed for the XY speed range
slider for the Motion Keypad (yellow icon). [default=0.1]
machine.max_xy_speed_tooltip:
The the highest speed for the XY speed range
slider for the Motion Keypad (yellow icon). [default=6.0]
machine.z_speed_fast_tooltip:
The "fast" Z speed used when driving the tool with the
Motion Keypad (yellow icon). [ << >> ]
machine.z_speed_slow_tooltip:
The "slow" Z speed used when driving the tool with the
Motion Keypad (yellow icon). [ < > ]
machine.xy_increment_tooltip:
The initial "Fixed Distance" move in the X or Y axis
for each "tap" when in Fixed mode. Also the smallest
move attainable with a quick keytap in continuous motion
mode. [default start = 0.05]
machine.z_increment_tooltip:
The initial "Fixed Distance" move in the Z axis for each
"tap" when in Fixed mode. Also the smallest move attainable
with a quick keytap in continuous motion mode. [default start = 0.005]
machine.abc_increment_tooltip:
The initial "Fixed Distance" move in the A, B or C axis for each
"tap" when in Fixed mode. Also the smallest move attainable with a
quick keytap in continuous motion mode. [default start = 1.0]
machine.xy_max_jerk_tooltip: XY acceleration for the Motion Keypad. [default=50]
machine.z_max_jerk_tooltip: Z acceleration for the Motion Keypad. [default=50]
machine.fixed_move_delay_tooltip:
How long a button must be held before continuous motion
starts (in milliseconds). A quick tap shorter than this
triggers a fixed-distance nudge instead. Set to 0 to
disable tap-nudge and get instant continuous motion.
[default=150]
machine.use_auth_button_tooltip:
If checked, the tool will require "authorization" before
executing a motion command originating from a wireless
or wired network. Authorization is issued by pressing the
authorization button at the tool.
Authorization insures that only an individual present at the
is able to start it in motion. [default=checked]
machine.lockout_scope_tooltip:
Which actions require the authorize button press.
"Files and manual control" gates everything (the original
behavior). "File runs only" lets the on-screen manual
controls and keyboard jog work without pressing authorize
first, while still requiring it before any file, macro, or
editor code is allowed to run. [default=Files and manual control]
machine.lockout_scope_file_only: File runs only (manual controls always available)
machine.auth_button_input_tooltip:
The input line to which the authorization button is connected.
If the tool is configured to require authorization, this
input must be pressed before any motion command can be
executed. [default=5]
machine.auth_timeout_tooltip:
The amount of time the tool remains authorized for use after
authorization has been issued with the authorization button.
Tool authorization is used to confirm that you are physically
present and working at the tool before executing a motion
command arriving over the wireless or wired network. [default=30]
machine.ap_button_input_tooltip:
The input to which the AP mode toggle button is connected.
Holding down the AP mode button for the required duration
causes the tool to enter AP mode, in which it becomes
a wireless access point that can be connected to directly.
If AP mode is already active, holding the button turns it off.
See: FabMo's network manager (Wifi icon at upper left).
[default=No AP Mode Button]
machine.spindle_setup_tooltip:
Detects the USB-to-RS485 adapter, identifies the connected
VFD by probing known register layouts, and installs the
matching profile so spindle RPM control is live.
Run this after plugging in a new spindle controller.
machine.spindle_status_initial: Click “Refresh Detection” to see what’s connected.

ghost_modal.title: Ghost / Preview Run
ghost_modal.intro: Preview the job without cutting. The original job stays queued for a real run afterward.
ghost_modal.trace_help: Drives around the perimeter of the cut at the configured jog rate without changing Z. You'll be prompted to set a safe Z before motion begins.

repeat_modal.intro: After this job finishes, requeue and run it again. Set a total run count, or leave blank to repeat until you stop it manually.
repeat_modal.count_label: Total times to run (blank = until canceled):

pending.up_next: Next Part(s) to Cut ... drag to change order

transforms.heading: Transforms
transforms.heading_tooltip:
Transforms can be used to modify a job to be run.
When enabled, the job will be Scaled in X, Y, or Z and
it may be Offset, Rotated, or an X or Y Shear applied.
Each enabled transform will be applied.

Order of operations: Rotation, Shear, Scale, Offset.
The effect of transforms can be seen in the preview.
(*In a g-code file that contains arcs, axes must be scaled
equally in X and Y.)
transforms.scale_tooltip:
Each axis may be proportionally scaled.
Scaling can be applied about a point in the axis;
the default Scale Point is 0 in each axis.
(*In a gcode file that contains arcs, axes must be scaled
equally in X and Y.)
transforms.offset_tooltip: Positions Offsets can be applied in each axis.
transforms.rotation_tooltip: A Job can be rotated about an X / Y point.
transforms.shear_tooltip: Applying a shear will slant the Job along an axis.
transforms.leveler_tooltip: The Leveler, using a Z-height point cloud map, adjusts the Z height across the Job to compensate for uneven surfaces.

settings.title: Settings
settings.grid_step: Grid Step (in current machine display units)
settings.table_zzoff: Table Offset from Z-Zero (in current machine display units)
settings.lookahead: Live Sync Buffer Compensation (SBP: SBP commands; GCode: gcode lines)
settings.show_pc: Show Point Cloud Shape for Leveler

help.title: Preview Help
help.key_i: Set Cut Start at current timeline position.
help.key_o: Set Cut Stop at current timeline position.
help.key_shift_arrows: Skip to the previous plunge / next retract.
help.key_esc: Close any open dialog (Settings, Help, etc.).
help.view_x: Snap to view down the X axis.
help.view_y: Snap to view down the Y axis.
help.view_z: Snap to view down the Z axis (top-down).
help.skip: Skip to previous plunge / next retract.
help.timeline_d: Drag to scrub through the path.
help.set_cut_d: Mark in/out points on the timeline. Used to play, run, or submit only a trimmed range. The × clears them.
help.path_mat_d: Quick toggles to show or hide the toolpath lines and the material simulation surface.
help.ops_intro: Files compiled from SBP or with VCarve/ShopBot operation comments expose each operation as a checkable row. Use the panel to override tool defaults, run only what you need, or submit a trimmed/selected job.
help.ops_setup_d: Sets the file-wide defaults: Material thickness, Z origin (Material Surface or Table Surface), and the Default tool (Flat / Ball / V-Bit, plus diameter and V-bit angle). Changes here re-render the material simulation.
help.ops_per_d: Each operation row has its own bit type, diameter, and (for V-bit) angle. Editing these overrides the Setup default for that operation only.
help.ops_full_d: Submits the entire file as loaded.
help.ops_trim_d: Run or submit only the range between Cut Start and Cut Stop (set with I / O or the bottom-bar buttons).
help.ops_sel_d: Run or submit only the operations whose checkboxes are ticked. The All link toggles every operation at once.
help.ar_intro: When a machine camera is detected and table corners have been calibrated, two extra toggles appear on the bottom bar:
help.ar_d: Overlays the toolpath preview on the live camera feed using the calibrated camera pose, so the rendered path appears in the same perspective as the real table.
help.top_d: Un-warped top-down view of the live table. The camera image is warped to a true overhead, with the toolpath rendered over it — useful for spotting clamps or fixtures the path would collide with.
help.recal_d: Re-marks the four table corners against the live video. Drag each handle (TL, TR, BR, BL) onto the corresponding corner of the table, then Save. Calibration is stored in the machine config and shared between AR and Top modes.
help.s_speed_d: Playback speed relative to real time (1/16x – 16x).
help.s_bound_d: Temporarily changes units (inches vs mm) for the file boundary indicators. A unit setting inside the file overrides the machine setting for motion, but does not change the machine’s display units.
help.s_grid_d: Grid step size in the current machine units.
help.s_zzoff_d: How far the table sits below the Z-zero plane. If you zero at the table, set 0. If you zero on top of 0.75" stock, set −0.75.
help.s_look_d: How many planner-buffered commands to skip ahead in the preview so the on-screen position tracks the actual machine more closely (SBP: SBP commands; GCode: gcode lines).
help.s_disp_d: Show or hide grid, machine table, dimensions, axes, tool cone, and point-cloud / wireframe overlays.
help.s_reset_d: Clears any cut material from the simulation surface and re-initializes a fresh stock block.
help.s_pc_d: Transparency of the leveler point-cloud overlay.
help.s_mat_d: Transparency of the simulated stock block.
help.s_light_d: Direction of the scene light. Adjust for better depth shading on the cut surface.
help.s_res_d: Height-map grid resolution for the material simulation. Higher is more accurate but slower to compute and re-render.

notify.webgl_disabled: WebGL is not enabled. Impossible to preview.
notify.set_inout_first: Set In and/or Out points first
notify.extract_failed: Could not extract selection from file

wifi.networks_available_tooltip: Wifi networks available to your tool. Click on SSID name to set up a new wifi interface to access your tool.

interfaces.heading: Access Interfaces
interfaces.tooltip: Your tool is connected to each of these interfaces for access. The favored interface access is at the top of the list and is shown on the R-Pi screen (if available). (if attached). To remove an interface, click on its name.
interfaces.info_ethernet: Ethernet: a LAN or direct PC connection

dialog.connect_success_description:
Access your tool on Wifi network:
        {name} at IP: {address}
dialog.remove_ethernet_description: To remove a LAN or direct PC interface; disconnect the Ethernet cable from your tool. Always allow 10 seconds before reconnecting another cable.
dialog.disconnect_wifi_title: Disconnect and forget this Wifi Interface ?
dialog.turn_off_wifi_warning: WARNING! This action also turns off AP Mode and IP address display. All Wifi Access will be eliminated!

notify.error_retrieve_network_info: failed to retrieve network information. Network management may not be available on your tool.
